column1: title#shloka_in_sanskrit#transliteration_en#translation_en#word_by_word_en#audio_url

BG 13.B#ॐ श्री परमात्मने नमः\nअथ त्रयोदशोऽध्यायः क्षेत्र क्षेत्रज्ञ विभाग योगः#om sri paramatmane namaha\natha trayodashodhyayaha kshetra kshetrajna vibhaga yogaha#Salutations to the most auspicious divine in all. Henceforth
column2: the thirteenth chapter named Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga#om sri paramatmane—the most auspicious divine in all; namaha—salutations; atha—henceforth; trayodashodhyayaha—thirteenth chapter; kshetra kshetrajna vibhaga yogaha—Yoga of the field and the knower of the field

column1:
BG 13.1#अर्जुन उवाच |
प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च |
एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव ||1||* 1#arjuna uvācha
prakṛitiṁ puruṣhaṁ chaiva kṣhetraṁ kṣhetra-jñam eva cha
etad veditum ichchhāmi jñānaṁ jñeyaṁ cha keśhava#Arjun said
column2: “O Keshav
column3: I wish to understand what are prakṛiti and puruṣh
column4: and what are kṣhetra and kṣhetrajña? I also wish to know what is true knowledge
column5: and what is the goal of this knowledge?#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said; prakṛitim—material nature; puruṣham—the enjoyer; cha—and; eva—indeed; kṣhetram—the field of activities; kṣhetra-jñam—the knower of the field; eva—even; cha—also; etat—this; veditum—to know; ichchhāmi—I wish; jñānam—knowledge; jñeyam—the goal of knowledge; cha—and; keśhava—Krishna
column6: the killer of the demon named Keshi

column1:
BG 13.2#श्रीभगवानुवाच |
इदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते |
एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहु: क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विद: ||2||#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
idaṁ śharīraṁ kaunteya kṣhetram ity abhidhīyate
etad yo vetti taṁ prāhuḥ kṣhetra-jña iti tad-vidaḥ#The Supreme Divine Lord said: O Arjun
column2: this body is termed as kṣhetra (the field of activities)
column3: and the one who knows this body is called kṣhetrajña (the knower of the field) by the sages who discern the truth about both.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Supreme Divine Lord said; idam—this; śharīram—body; kaunteya—Arjun
column4: the son of Kunti; kṣhetram—the field of activities; iti—thus; abhidhīyate—is termed as; etat—this; yaḥ—one who; vetti—knows; tam—that person; prāhuḥ—is called; kṣhetra-jñaḥ—the knower of the field; iti—thus; tat-vidaḥ—those who discern the truth

column1: BG 13.3#क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत |\nक्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम ||3||#kṣhetra-jñaṁ chāpi māṁ viddhi sarva-kṣhetreṣhu bhārata\nkṣhetra-kṣhetrajñayor jñānaṁ yat taj jñānaṁ mataṁ mama#O scion of Bharat
column2: I am also the knower of all the individual fields of activity. The understanding of the body as the field of activities
column3: and the soul and God as the knowers of the field
column4: this I hold to be true knowledge.#kṣhetra-jñam—the knower of the field; cha—also; api—only; mām—me; viddhi—know; sarva—all; kṣhetreṣhu—in individual fields of activities; bhārata—scion of Bharat; kṣhetra—the field of activities; kṣhetra-jñayoḥ—of the knower of the field; jñānam—understanding of; yat—which; tat—that; jñānam—knowledge; matam—opinion; mama—my

column1: BG 13.4#तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक्च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत् |\nस च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे शृणु ||4||#tat kṣhetraṁ yach cha yādṛik cha yad-vikāri yataśh cha yat\nsa cha yo yat-prabhāvaśh cha tat samāsena me śhṛiṇu#Listen and I will explain to you what that field is and what its nature is. I will also explain how change takes place within it
column2: from what it was created
column3: who the knower of the field of activities is
column4: and what his powers are.#tat—that; kṣhetram—field of activities; yat—what; cha—and; yādṛik—its nature; cha—and; yat-vikāri—how change takes place in it; yataḥ—from what; cha—also; yat—what; saḥ—he; cha—also; yaḥ—who; yat-prabhāvaḥ—what his powers are; cha—and; tat—that; samāsena—in summary; me—from me; śhṛiṇu—listen

column1: BG 13.5#ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधै: पृथक् |\nब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भिर्विनिश्चितै: ||5||#ṛiṣhibhir bahudhā gītaṁ chhandobhir vividhaiḥ pṛithak\nbrahma-sūtra-padaiśh chaiva hetumadbhir viniśhchitaiḥ#Great sages have sung the truth about the field and the knower of the field in manifold ways. It has been stated in various Vedic hymns
column2: and especially revealed in the Brahma Sūtra
column3: with sound logic and conclusive evidence.#ṛiṣhibhiḥ—by great sages; bahudhā—in manifold ways; gītam—sung; chhandobhiḥ—in Vedic hymns; vividhaiḥ—various; pṛithak—various; brahma-sūtra—the Brahma Sūtra; padaiḥ—by the hymns; cha—and; eva—especially; hetu-madbhiḥ—with logic; viniśhchitaiḥ—conclusive evidence

column1: BG 13.6#महाभूतान्यङ्ककारो बुद्धिरव्यक्त मेव च |\nइन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचरा: ||6||#mahā-bhūtāny ahankāro buddhir avyaktam eva cha\nindriyāṇi daśhaikaṁ cha pañcha chendriya-gocharāḥ#The field of activities is composed of the five great elements
column2: the ego
column3: the intellect
column8: and the five objects of the senses.#mahā-bhūtāni—the (five) great elements; ahankāraḥ—the ego; buddhiḥ—the intellect; avyaktam—the unmanifested primordial matter; eva—indeed; cha—and; indriyāṇi—the senses; daśha-ekam—eleven; cha—and; pañcha—five; cha—and; indriya-go-charāḥ—the (five) objects of the senses;

column1: BG 13.7#इच्छा द्वेष: सुखं दु:खं सङ्घातश्चेतना धृति: |\nएतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम् ||7||#ichchhā dveṣhaḥ sukhaṁ duḥkhaṁ saṅghātaśh chetanā dhṛitiḥ\netat kṣhetraṁ samāsena sa-vikāram udāhṛitam#Desire and aversion
column2: happiness and misery
column3: the body
column5: and the will—all these comprise the field and its modifications.#ichchhā—desire; dveṣhaḥ—aversion; sukham—happiness; duḥkham—misery; saṅghātaḥ—the aggregate; chetanā—the consciousness; dhṛitiḥ—the will; etat—all these; kṣhetram—the field of activities; samāsena—comprise of; sa-vikāram—with modifications; udāhṛitam—are said

column1:
BG 13.8#अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम् |
आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रह: ||8||
इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च |
जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदु:खदोषानुदर्शनम् ||9||
असक्तिरनभिष्वङ्ग: पुत्रदारगृहादिषु |
नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु ||10||
मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी |
विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि ||11||
अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम् |
एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोऽन्यथा ||12||#amānitvam adambhitvam ahinsā kṣhāntir ārjavam
āchāryopāsanaṁ śhauchaṁ sthairyam ātma-vinigrahaḥ
indriyārtheṣhu vairāgyam anahankāra eva cha
janma-mṛityu-jarā-vyādhi-duḥkha-doṣhānudarśhanam
asaktir anabhiṣhvaṅgaḥ putra-dāra-gṛihādiṣhu
nityaṁ cha sama-chittatvam iṣhṭāniṣhṭopapattiṣhu
mayi chānanya-yogena bhaktir avyabhichāriṇī
vivikta-deśha-sevitvam aratir jana-sansadi
adhyātma-jñāna-nityatvaṁ tattva-jñānārtha-darśhanam
etaj jñānam iti proktam ajñānaṁ yad ato ’nyathā#BG 13.8-12:#amānitvam—humbleness; adambhitvam—freedom from hypocrisy; ahinsā—non-violence; kṣhāntiḥ—forgiveness; ārjavam—simplicity; āchārya-upāsanam—service of the Guru; śhaucham—cleanliness of body and mind; sthairyam—steadfastness; ātma-vinigrahaḥ—self-control; indriya-artheṣhu—toward objects of the senses; vairāgyam—dispassion; anahankāraḥ—absence of egotism; eva cha—and also; janma—of birth; mṛityu—death; jarā—old age; vyādhi—disease; duḥkha—evils; doṣha—faults; anudarśhanam—perception; asaktiḥ—non-attachment; anabhiṣhvaṅgaḥ—absence of craving; putra—children; dāra—spouse; gṛiha-ādiṣhu—home
column2: etc; nityam—constant; cha—and; sama-chittatvam—even-mindedness; iṣhṭa—the desirable; aniṣhṭa—undesirable; upapattiṣhu—having obtained; mayi—toward Me; cha—also; ananya-yogena—exclusively united; bhaktiḥ—devotion; avyabhichāriṇī—constant; vivikta—solitary; deśha—places; sevitvam—inclination for; aratiḥ—aversion; jana-sansadi—for mundane society; adhyātma—spiritual; jñāna—knowledge; nityatvam—constancy; tattva-jñāna—knowledge of spiritual principles; artha—for; darśhanam—philosophy; etat—all this; jñānam—knowledge; iti—thus; proktam—declared; ajñānam—ignorance; yat—what; ataḥ—to this; anyathā—contrary

BG 13.12#अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम् |
आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रह: ||8||
इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च |
जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदु:खदोषानुदर्शनम् ||9||
असक्तिरनभिष्वङ्ग: पुत्रदारगृहादिषु |
नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु ||10||
मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी |
विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि ||11||
अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम् |
एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोऽन्यथा ||12||#amānitvam adambhitvam ahinsā kṣhāntir ārjavam
āchāryopāsanaṁ śhauchaṁ sthairyam ātma-vinigrahaḥ
indriyārtheṣhu vairāgyam anahankāra eva cha
janma-mṛityu-jarā-vyādhi-duḥkha-doṣhānudarśhanam
asaktir anabhiṣhvaṅgaḥ putra-dāra-gṛihādiṣhu
nityaṁ cha sama-chittatvam iṣhṭāniṣhṭopapattiṣhu
mayi chānanya-yogena bhaktir avyabhichāriṇī
vivikta-deśha-sevitvam aratir jana-sansadi
adhyātma-jñāna-nityatvaṁ tattva-jñānārtha-darśhanam
etaj jñānam iti proktam ajñānaṁ yad ato ’nyathā#BG 13.8-12:#amānitvam—humbleness; adambhitvam—freedom from hypocrisy; ahinsā—non-violence; kṣhāntiḥ—forgiveness; ārjavam—simplicity; āchārya-upāsanam—service of the Guru; śhaucham—cleanliness of body and mind; sthairyam—steadfastness; ātma-vinigrahaḥ—self-control; indriya-artheṣhu—toward objects of the senses; vairāgyam—dispassion; anahankāraḥ—absence of egotism; eva cha—and also; janma—of birth; mṛityu—death; jarā—old age; vyādhi—disease; duḥkha—evils; doṣha—faults; anudarśhanam—perception; asaktiḥ—non-attachment; anabhiṣhvaṅgaḥ—absence of craving; putra—children; dāra—spouse; gṛiha-ādiṣhu—home
column2: etc; nityam—constant; cha—and; sama-chittatvam—even-mindedness; iṣhṭa—the desirable; aniṣhṭa—undesirable; upapattiṣhu—having obtained; mayi—toward Me; cha—also; ananya-yogena—exclusively united; bhaktiḥ—devotion; avyabhichāriṇī—constant; vivikta—solitary; deśha—places; sevitvam—inclination for; aratiḥ—aversion; jana-sansadi—for mundane society; adhyātma—spiritual; jñāna—knowledge; nityatvam—constancy; tattva-jñāna—knowledge of spiritual principles; artha—for; darśhanam—philosophy; etat—all this; jñānam—knowledge; iti—thus; proktam—declared; ajñānam—ignorance; yat—what; ataḥ—to this; anyathā—contrary

column1: BG 13.13#ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वामृतमश्रुते |\nअनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते ||13||#jñeyaṁ yat tat pravakṣhyāmi yaj jñātvāmṛitam aśhnute\nanādi mat-paraṁ brahma na sat tan nāsad uchyate#I shall now reveal to you that which ought to be known
column2: and by knowing which
column3: one attains immortality. It is the beginningless Brahman
column4: which lies beyond existence and non-existence.#jñeyam—ought to be known; yat—which; tat—that; pravakṣhyāmi—I shall now reveal; yat—which; jñātvā—knowing; amṛitam—immortality; aśhnute—one achieves; anādi—beginningless; mat-param—subordinate to Me; brahma—Brahman; na—not; sat—existent; tat—that; na—not; asat—non-existent; uchyate—is called

column1: BG 13.14#सर्वत: पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम् |\nसर्वत: श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति ||14||#sarvataḥ pāṇi-pādaṁ tat sarvato ’kṣhi-śhiro-mukham\nsarvataḥ śhrutimal loke sarvam āvṛitya tiṣhṭhati#Everywhere are His hands and feet
column2: eyes
column3: heads
column4: and faces. His ears too are in all places
column5: for He pervades everything in the universe.#sarvataḥ—everywhere; pāṇi—hands; pādam—feet; tat—that; sarvataḥ—everywhere; akṣhi—eyes; śhiraḥ—heads; mukham—faces; sarvataḥ—everywhere; śhruti-mat—having ears; loke—in the universe; sarvam—everything; āvṛitya—pervades; tiṣhṭhati—exists

column1: BG 13.15#सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम् |\nअसक्तं सर्वभृच्चैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च ||15||#sarvendriya-guṇābhāsaṁ sarvendriya-vivarjitam\nasaktaṁ sarva-bhṛich chaiva nirguṇaṁ guṇa-bhoktṛi cha#Though He perceives all sense-objects
column2: yet He is devoid of the senses. He is unattached to everything
column3: and yet He is the sustainer of all. Although He is without attributes
column4: yet He is the enjoyer of the three modes of material nature.#sarva—all; indriya—senses; guṇa—sense-objects; ābhāsam—the perciever; sarva—all; indriya—senses; vivarjitam—devoid of; asaktam—unattached; sarva-bhṛit—the sustainer of all; cha—yet; eva—indeed; nirguṇam—beyond the three modes of material nature; guṇa-bhoktṛi—the enjoyer of the three modes of material nature; cha—although

column1: BG 13.16#बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च |\nसूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत् ||16||#bahir antaśh cha bhūtānām acharaṁ charam eva cha\nsūkṣhmatvāt tad avijñeyaṁ dūra-sthaṁ chāntike cha tat#He exists outside and inside all living beings
column2: those that are moving and not moving. He is subtle
column3: and hence
column4: He is incomprehensible. He is very far
column5: but He is also very near.#bahiḥ—outside; antaḥ—inside; cha—and; bhūtānām—all living beings; acharam—not moving; charam—moving; eva—indeed; cha—and; sūkṣhmatvāt—due to subtlety; tat—he; avijñeyam—incomprehensible; dūra-stham—very far away; cha—and; antike—very near; cha—also; tat—He

column1: BG 13.17#अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम् |\nभूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च ||17||#avibhaktaṁ cha bhūteṣhu vibhaktam iva cha sthitam\nbhūta-bhartṛi cha taj jñeyaṁ grasiṣhṇu prabhaviṣhṇu cha#He is indivisible
column2: yet He appears to be divided amongst living beings. Know the Supreme Entity to be the Sustainer
column3: Annihilator
column4: and Creator of all beings.#avibhaktam—indivisible; cha—although; bhūteṣhu—amongst living beings; vibhaktam—divided; iva—apparently; cha—yet; sthitam—situated; bhūta-bhartṛi—the Sustainer of all beings; cha—also; tat—that; jñeyam—to be known; grasiṣhṇu—the Annihilator; prabhaviṣhṇu—the Creator; cha—and

column1: BG 13.18#ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमस: परमुच्यते |\nज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम् ||18||#jyotiṣhām api taj jyotis tamasaḥ param uchyate\njñānaṁ jñeyaṁ jñāna-gamyaṁ hṛidi sarvasya viṣhṭhitam#He is the source of light in all luminaries
column2: and is entirely beyond the darkness of ignorance. He is knowledge
column3: the object of knowledge
column4: and the goal of knowledge. He dwells within the hearts of all living beings.#jyotiṣhām—in all luminarie; api—and; tat—that; jyotiḥ—the source of light; tamasaḥ—the darkness; param—beyond; uchyate—is said (to be); jñānam—knowledge; jñeyam—the object of knowledge; jñāna-gamyam—the goal of knowledge; hṛidi—within the heart; sarvasya—of all living beings; viṣhṭhitam—dwells

column1: BG 13.19#इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासत: |\nमद्भक्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भावायोपपद्यते ||19||#iti kṣhetraṁ tathā jñānaṁ jñeyaṁ choktaṁ samāsataḥ\nmad-bhakta etad vijñāya mad-bhāvāyopapadyate#I have thus revealed to you the nature of the field
column2: the meaning of knowledge
column3: and the object of knowledge. Only My devotees can understand this in reality
column5: they attain My divine nature.#iti—thus; kṣhetram—the nature of the field; tathā—and; jñānam—the meaning of knowledge; jñeyam—the object of knowledge; cha—and; uktam—revealed; samāsataḥ—in summary; mad-bhaktaḥ—my devotees; etat—this; vijñāya—having understood; mad-bhāvāya—my divine nature; upapadyate—attain

column1: BG 13.20#प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव विद्ध्यनादी उभावपि |\nविकारांश्च गुणांश्चैव विद्धि प्रकृतिसम्भवान् ||20||#prakṛitiṁ puruṣhaṁ chaiva viddhy anādī ubhāv api\nvikārānśh cha guṇānśh chaiva viddhi prakṛiti-sambhavān#Know that prakṛiti (material nature) and puruṣh (the individual souls) are both beginningless. Also know that all transformations of the body and the three modes of nature are produced by material energy.#prakṛitim—material nature; puruṣham—the individual souls; cha—and; eva—indeed; viddhi—know; anādī—beginningless; ubhau—both; api—and; vikārān—transformations (of the body); cha—also; guṇān—the three modes of nature; cha—and; eva—indeed; viddhi—know; prakṛiti—material energy; sambhavān—produced by

BG 13.21#कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे हेतु: प्रकृतिरुच्यते |\nपुरुष: सुखदु:खानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते ||21||#kārya-kāraṇa-kartṛitve hetuḥ prakṛitir uchyate\npuruṣhaḥ sukha-duḥkhānāṁ bhoktṛitve hetur uchyate#In the matter of creation
column2: the material energy is responsible for cause and effect; in the matter of experiencing happiness and distress
column3: the individual soul is declared responsible.#kārya—effect; kāraṇa—cause; kartṛitve—in the matter of creation; hetuḥ—the medium; prakṛitiḥ—the material energy; uchyate—is said to be; puruṣhaḥ—the individual soul; sukha-duḥkhānām—of happiness and distress; bhoktṛitve—in experiencing; hetuḥ—is responsible; uchyate—is said to be

column1: BG 13.22#पुरुष: प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङक्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान् |\nकारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु ||22||#puruṣhaḥ prakṛiti-stho hi bhuṅkte prakṛiti-jān guṇān\nkāraṇaṁ guṇa-saṅgo ’sya sad-asad-yoni-janmasu#When the puruṣh (individual soul) seated in prakṛiti (the material energy) desires to enjoy the three guṇas
column2: attachment to them becomes the cause of its birth in superior and inferior wombs.#puruṣhaḥ—the individual soul; prakṛiti-sthaḥ—seated in the material energy; hi—indeed; bhuṅkte—desires to enjoy; prakṛiti-jān—produced by the material energy; guṇān—the three modes of nature; kāraṇam—the cause; guṇa-saṅgaḥ—the attachment (to three guṇas); asya—of its; sat-asat-yoni—in superior and inferior wombs; janmasu—of birth

column1: BG 13.23#उपद्रष्टानुमन्ता च भर्ता भोक्ता महेश्वर: |\nपरमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुष: पर: ||23||#upadraṣhṭānumantā cha bhartā bhoktā maheśhvaraḥ\nparamātmeti chāpy ukto dehe ’smin puruṣhaḥ paraḥ#Within the body also resides the Supreme Lord. He is said to be the Witness
column2: the Permitter
column3: the Supporter
column6: and the Paramātmā (Supreme Soul).#upadraṣhṭā—the witness; anumantā—the permitter; cha—and; bhartā—the supporter; bhoktā—the transcendental enjoyer; mahā-īśhvaraḥ—the ultimate controller; parama-ātmā—Supreme Soul; iti—that; cha api—and also; uktaḥ—is said; dehe—within the body; asmin—this; puruṣhaḥ paraḥ—the Supreme Lord

column1: BG 13.24#य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणै: सह |\nसर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते ||24||#ya evaṁ vetti puruṣhaṁ prakṛitiṁ cha guṇaiḥ saha\nsarvathā vartamāno ’pi na sa bhūyo ’bhijāyate#Those who understand the truth about Supreme Soul
column2: the individual soul
column3: material nature
column4: and the interaction of the three modes of nature will not take birth here again. They will be liberated regardless of their present condition.#yaḥ—who; evam—thus; vetti—understand; puruṣham—Puruṣh; prakṛitim—the material nature; cha—and; guṇaiḥ—the three modes of nature; saha—with; sarvathā—in every way; vartamānaḥ—situated; api—although; na—not; saḥ—they; bhūyaḥ—again; abhijāyate—take birth

column1: BG 13.25#ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना |\nअन्ये साङ् ख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे ||25||#dhyānenātmani paśhyanti kechid ātmānam ātmanā\nanye sānkhyena yogena karma-yogena chāpare#Some try to perceive the Supreme Soul within their hearts through meditation
column2: and others try to do so through the cultivation of knowledge
column3: while still others strive to attain that realization by the path of action.#dhyānena—through meditation; ātmani—within one’s heart; paśhyanti—perceive; kechit—some; ātmānam—the Supreme soul; ātmanā—by the mind; anye—others; sānkhyena—through cultivation of knowledge; yogena—the yog system; karma-yogena—union with God with through path of action; cha—and; apare—others

column1: BG 13.26#अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्त: श्रुत्वान्येभ्य उपासते |\nतेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणा: ||26||#anye tv evam ajānantaḥ śhrutvānyebhya upāsate\nte ’pi chātitaranty eva mṛityuṁ śhruti-parāyaṇāḥ#There are still others who are unaware of these spiritual paths
column2: but they hear from others and begin worshipping the Supreme Lord. By such devotion to hearing from saints
column3: they too can gradually cross over the ocean of birth and death.#anye—others; tu—still; evam—thus; ajānantaḥ—those who are unaware (of spiritual paths); śhrutvā—by hearing; anyebhyaḥ—from others; upāsate—begin to worship; te—they; api—also; cha—and; atitaranti—cross over; eva—even; mṛityum—death; śhruti-parāyaṇāḥ—devotion to hearing (from saints)

column1: BG 13.27#यावत्सञ्जायते किञ्चित्सत्वं स्थावरजङ्गमम् |\nक्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञसंयोगात्तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ ||27||#yāvat sañjāyate kiñchit sattvaṁ sthāvara-jaṅgamam\nkṣhetra-kṣhetrajña-sanyogāt tad viddhi bharatarṣhabha#O best of the Bharatas
column2: whatever moving or unmoving being you see in existence
column3: know it to be a combination of the field of activities and the knower of the field.#yāvat—whatever; sañjāyate—manifesting; kiñchit—anything; sattvam—being; sthāvara—unmoving; jaṅgamam—moving; kṣhetra—field of activities; kṣhetra-jña—knower of the field; sanyogāt—combination of; tat—that; viddhi—know; bharata-ṛiṣhabha—best of the Bharatas

column1: BG 13.28#समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम् |\nविनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं य: पश्यति स पश्यति ||28||#samaṁ sarveṣhu bhūteṣhu tiṣhṭhantaṁ parameśhvaram\nvinaśhyatsv avinaśhyantaṁ yaḥ paśhyati sa paśhyati#They alone truly see
column2: who perceive the Paramātmā (Supreme Soul) accompanying the soul in all beings
column3: and who understand both to be imperishable in this perishable body.#samam—equally; sarveṣhu—in all; bhūteṣhu—beings; tiṣhṭhan-tam—accompanying; parama-īśhvaram—Supreme Soul; vinaśhyatsu—amongst the perishable; avinaśhyantam—the imperishable; yaḥ—who; paśhyati—see; saḥ—they; paśhyati—perceive

column1: BG 13.29#समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम् |\nन हिनस्त्यात्मनात्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम् ||29||#samaṁ paśhyan hi sarvatra samavasthitam īśhvaram\nna hinasty ātmanātmānaṁ tato yāti parāṁ gatim#Those
column2: who see God as the Supreme Soul equally present everywhere and in all living beings
column3: do not degrade themselves by their mind. Thereby
column4: they reach the supreme destination.#samam—equally; paśhyan—see; hi—indeed; sarvatra—everywhere; samavasthitam—equally present; īśhvaram—God as the Supreme soul; na—do not; hinasti—degrade; ātmanā—by one’s mind; ātmānam—the self; tataḥ—thereby; yāti—reach; parām—the supreme; gatim—destination

column1: BG 13.30#प्रकृत्यैव च कर्माणि क्रियमाणानि सर्वश: |\nय: पश्यति तथात्मानमकर्तारं स पश्यति ||30||#prakṛityaiva cha karmāṇi kriyamāṇāni sarvaśhaḥ\nyaḥ paśhyati tathātmānam akartāraṁ sa paśhyati#They alone truly see who understand that all actions (of the body) are performed by material nature
column2: while the embodied soul actually does nothing.#prakṛityā—by material nature; eva—truly; cha—also; karmāṇi—actions; kriyamāṇāni—are performed; sarvaśhaḥ—all; yaḥ—who; paśhyati—see; tathā—also; ātmānam—(embodied) soul; akartāram—actionless; saḥ—they; paśhyati—see

column1: BG 13.31#यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति |\nतत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते तदा ||31||#yadā bhūta-pṛithag-bhāvam eka-stham anupaśhyati\ntata eva cha vistāraṁ brahma sampadyate tadā#When they see the diverse variety of living beings situated in the same material nature
column2: and understand all of them to be born from it
column3: they attain the realization of the Brahman.#yadā—when; bhūta—living entities; pṛithak-bhāvam—diverse variety; eka-stham—situated in the same place; anupaśhyati—see; tataḥ—thereafter; eva—indeed; cha—and; vistāram—born from; brahma—Brahman; sampadyate—(they) attain; tadā—then

column1: BG 13.32#अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्यय: |\nशरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते ||32||#anāditvān nirguṇatvāt paramātmāyam avyayaḥ\nśharīra-stho ’pi kaunteya na karoti na lipyate#The Supreme Soul is imperishable
column2: without beginning
column3: and devoid of any material qualities
column4: O son of Kunti. Although situated within the body
column6: nor is It tainted by material energy.#anāditvāt—being without beginning; nirguṇatvāt—being devoid of any material qualities; parama—the Supreme; ātmā—soul; ayam—this; avyayaḥ—imperishable; śharīra-sthaḥ—dwelling in the body; api—although; kaunteya—Arjun
column7: the the son of Kunti; na—neither; karoti—acts; na—nor; lipyate—is tainted

column1: BG 13.33#यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते |\nसर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथात्मा नोपलिप्यते ||33||#yathā sarva-gataṁ saukṣhmyād ākāśhaṁ nopalipyate\nsarvatrāvasthito dehe tathātmā nopalipyate#Space holds everything within it
column2: but being subtle
column3: does not get contaminated by what it holds. Similarly
column4: though its consciousness pervades the body
column5: the soul is not affected by the attributes of the body.#yathā—as; sarva-gatam—all-pervading; saukṣhmyāt—due to subtlety; ākāśham—the space; na—not; upalipyate—is contaminated; sarvatra—everywhere; avasthitaḥ—situated; dehe—the body; tathā—similarly; ātmā—the soul; na—not; upalipyate—is contaminated

column1: BG 13.34#यथा प्रकाशयत्येक: कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रवि: |\nक्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत ||34||#yathā prakāśhayaty ekaḥ kṛitsnaṁ lokam imaṁ raviḥ\nkṣhetraṁ kṣhetrī tathā kṛitsnaṁ prakāśhayati bhārata#Just as one sun illumines the entire solar system
column2: so does the individual soul illumine the entire body (with consciousness).#yathā—as; prakāśhayati—illumines; ekaḥ—one; kṛitsnam—entire; lokam—solar system; imam—this; raviḥ—sun; kṣhetram—the body; kṣhetrī—the soul; tathā—so; kṛitsnam—entire; prakāśhayati—illumine; bhārata—Arjun
column3: the son of Bharat

column1: BG 13.35#क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा |\nभूतप्रकृतिमोक्षं च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम् ||35||#kṣhetra-kṣhetrajñayor evam antaraṁ jñāna-chakṣhuṣhā\nbhūta-prakṛiti-mokṣhaṁ cha ye vidur yānti te param#Those who perceive with the eyes of knowledge the difference between the body and the knower of the body
column2: and the process of release from material nature
column3: attain the supreme destination.#kṣhetra—the body; kṣhetra-jñayoḥ—of the knower of the body; evam—thus; antaram—the difference; jñāna-chakṣhuṣhā—with the eyes of knowledge; bhūta—the living entity; prakṛiti-mokṣham—release from material nature; cha—and; ye—who; viduḥ—know; yānti—approach; te—they; param—the Supreme

column1:
BG 13.E#ॐ तत् सत् इति श्रीमत् भगवत् गीतासु उपनिषत्सु
ब्रह्म-विद्यायां योग-शास्त्रे श्रीकृष्ण-अर्जुन-संवादे
क्षेत्र-क्षेत्रज्ञ-विभाग योगो नाम त्रयोदशोऽध्यायः#Om tat sat iti
Srimad Bhagavad Gitasu Upanishatsu
Brahma Vidyayam yogashastre Sri-Krishna-arujuna samvade
Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yogo Nama trayodashodhyaha#Om Truth Is. Thus in the Upanishads of the glorious Bhagavad Gita
column2: the science of the Eternal
column3: the scripture of Yoga
column4: the dialogue between Sri Krishna and Arjuna ends the Thirteenth discourse entitled The Yoga of the Field and the Knower of the Field.#Om—Om; tat—That; sat—truth; iti—is; Srimad—Auspicious one; Bhagavad—said by lord; Gitasu—song; Upanishatsu—upanishad; Brahma Vidyayam—contains knowledge of Brahman; yogashastre—contains technology of removing duality; Sri Krishna arujuna samvade—a dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna; Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yogo Nama—Yoga of the Field and the Knower of the Field; trayodashodhyaha—Thirteenth chapter
